extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE attacked alone with 1.4 miles to go on final cat-3 climb, held off Pogačar/Roglič chase for stage win and yellow jersey.
tactical_patterns: Van der Poel and Roglic positioned at back of peloton early, avoiding stress rather than front safety position.
Pogačar and Roglič both chased for time bonuses despite stage win unlikely, revealing GC primacy over stage hunting.
Deceuninck dominated closing kilometers, strongest team despite Alaphilippe's solo move suggesting pre-planned isolation tactic.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Won from 1.4 miles out after early crash, suggesting exceptional form despite world champion curse narrative
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: neutral: Sat at back early then struggled on final climb, different positioning philosophy than favorites
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primož: positive: Sprinted for third and time bonuses despite no stage win chance, prioritizing seconds early
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: Chased immediately when Roglič moved for bonuses, showing aggressive GC mindset from stage one
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: positive: Looked strong on final climb positioning despite crash earlier, better climbing form than expected
